title: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake

seo.title: Haemul Pajeon Recipe: Authentic Korean Seafood Pancake
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Haemul Pajeon, the crispy Korean seafood pancake, at home! This easy recipe brings the taste of Korea to your kitchen.
seo.h1: Authentic Haemul Pajeon: Korean Seafood Pancake Recipe
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Haemul Pajeon
seo.imageAlt: Crispy homemade Haemul Pajeon, a delicious Korean seafood pancake, served with dipping sauce.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a perfectly golden and crispy Haemul Pajeon, loaded with fresh seafood and green onions.

description: Haemul Pajeon is a beloved Korean savory pancake, celebrated for its crispy edges and tender interior packed with a delightful medley of fresh seafood and vibrant green onions. This traditional dish offers a perfect balance of textures and flavors, making it an irresistible appetizer or a light meal. Served with a tangy dipping sauce, it is a true taste of Korean comfort food.

ingredients.notes: shrimp, squid, mussels, scallops, cleaned and chopped

instructions.title: Prepare the Seafood and Vegetables
instructions.text: Clean and prepare your seafood. If using shrimp, peel and devein. Cut squid into rings or bite-sized pieces. Pat all seafood dry with paper towels to prevent excess moisture. Wash and trim green onions, cutting them into 3-4 inch lengths. Thinly slice the onion and red chili if using.

instructions.title: Mix the Batter
instructions.text: In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, cornstarch (or rice flour), salt, cold water, and the lightly beaten egg until a smooth batter forms. Do not overmix; a few small lumps are acceptable. The batter should be thick enough to coat the seafood and vegetables lightly.

instructions.title: Combine Ingredients
instructions.text: Add the prepared seafood, green onions, and sliced onion into the batter bowl. Gently fold to coat all ingredients evenly with the batter. Be careful not to break the seafood or green onions.

instructions.title: Cook the Pancake
instructions.text: Heat 2-3 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, pour about half of the batter mixture into the skillet, spreading it evenly to form a round pancake. Arrange some extra green onion stalks and seafood on top for presentation if desired, pressing them gently into the batter. Cook for 5-7 minutes until the bottom is golden brown and crispy.

instructions.title: Flip and Finish Cooking
instructions.text: Carefully flip the pancake using a large spatula. Add another tablespoon of oil around the edges of the pancake to ensure crispiness. Cook for another 5-7 minutes on the second side until it is also golden brown and cooked through. Repeat for the second pancake if making two.

instructions.title: Prepare Dipping Sauce and Serve
instructions.text: While the pancake cooks, whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, minced garlic, and toasted sesame seeds in a small bowl to make the dipping sauce. Once the Haemul Pajeon is cooked, transfer it to a cutting board, slice it into wedges, and serve immediately with the dipping sauce. Garnish with sliced red chili if desired.

tips: Use very cold water or anchovy broth for the batter; this helps create a crispier pancake by inhibiting gluten development.
For extra crispiness, use a combination of all-purpose flour and a small amount of rice flour or cornstarch in your batter.
Serve Haemul Pajeon immediately after cooking to enjoy its maximum crispiness. Leftovers can be reheated in a dry skillet or oven for a few minutes.
article:
Unleash your inner chef and embark on a culinary journey to Korea with one of its most beloved street food and restaurant staples: Haemul Pajeon. This savory Korean seafood pancake is a textural masterpiece, offering a delightful contrast between a crispy exterior, a tender, chewy interior, and generous chunks of fresh seafood and vibrant green onions. Perfect as an appetizer, a satisfying snack, or even a light meal, Haemul Pajeon is a dish that promises to captivate your senses and leave you craving more.

## What is Haemul Pajeon?

Haemul Pajeon, literally translating to "seafood green onion pancake," is a quintessential Korean comfort food. Unlike its sweeter counterparts found in other cuisines, this pancake is distinctly savory, packed with umami-rich ingredients. Its origins lie in the practical use of abundant local seafood and green onions, transformed into a hearty and flavorful dish. What sets Haemul Pajeon apart is its unique batter, often incorporating rice flour or cornstarch alongside wheat flour, which contributes to its signature crispy yet chewy texture. The star ingredients, fresh seafood and an abundance of green onions, are suspended within this delightful batter, pan-fried to a golden perfection. It’s a dish that embodies both simplicity and profound flavor, a true testament to Korean culinary artistry.

## Ingredients You'll Need

Creating an authentic Haemul Pajeon at home is surprisingly straightforward. Here’s a list of essential ingredients to get you started:

For the Batter:
*   1 cup all-purpose flour
*   1/4 cup rice flour or cornstarch (for extra crispiness)
*   1 teaspoon salt
*   1/2 teaspoon black pepper
*   1/4 teaspoon garlic powder (optional)
*   1 1/2 cups cold water (or anchovy broth for deeper flavor)
*   1 egg

For the Filling:
*   2 cups green onions, cut into 3-4 inch lengths (white and light green parts preferred)
*   1 cup assorted seafood, cleaned and chopped into bite-sized pieces (shrimp, squid, mussels, oysters, clams are popular choices)
*   1/2 small onion, thinly sliced (optional)
*   1/4 cup red bell pepper, thinly sliced (optional, for color and sweetness)
*   1-2 fresh red chilies, thinly sliced (optional, for heat)

For Frying:
*   Vegetable oil or neutral cooking oil

For the Dipping Sauce:
*   2 tablespoons soy sauce
*   1 tablespoon rice vinegar
*   1 teaspoon sesame oil
*   1/2 teaspoon gochugaru (Korean chili flakes, optional)
*   1/2 teaspoon toasted sesame seeds
*   1 clove garlic, minced (optional)

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Follow these steps to create your perfect Haemul Pajeon:

1.  **Prepare the Seafood and Vegetables**: If using frozen seafood, thaw it completely and pat dry. Chop all seafood into bite-sized pieces. Wash and trim the green onions, then cut them into 3-4 inch lengths. Slice any optional vegetables (onion, bell pepper, chili).
2.  **Make the Batter**: In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, rice flour (or cornstarch), salt, pepper, and garlic powder (if using). Gradually add the cold water (or broth) and egg, whisking until a smooth, lump-free batter forms. The consistency should be similar to a thin pancake batter – not too thick, not too watery.
3.  **Combine Ingredients**: Add the prepared green onions, seafood, and any other sliced vegetables to the batter. Gently mix until everything is evenly coated. Be careful not to overmix, as this can develop the gluten in the flour and make the pancake tough.
4.  **Heat the Pan**: Heat a large, non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Add a generous amount of vegetable oil, enough to coat the bottom of the pan and lightly fry the pancake (about 2-3 tablespoons). The oil should be shimmering but not smoking.
5.  **Cook the Pancake**: Pour about 1/2 to 1 cup of the batter mixture into the hot pan, spreading it evenly to form a round pancake, about 1/2 inch thick. You might need to cook in batches depending on your pan size. Ensure the seafood and green onions are well distributed.
6.  **Fry to Perfection**: Cook for 4-6 minutes on the first side, or until the bottom is golden brown and crispy, and the edges begin to set.
7.  **Flip and Finish**: Carefully flip the Haemul Pajeon using a wide spatula. Add a little more oil around the edges if needed. Cook for another 3-5 minutes on the second side, until golden brown and cooked through. The seafood should be opaque, and the green onions tender.
8.  **Repeat**: Transfer the cooked pancake to a cutting board. Repeat with the remaining batter, adding more oil as needed for each new pancake.

## Pro Tips for the Best Haemul Pajeon

Achieving that perfect balance of crispy and chewy in your Haemul Pajeon is an art, but these tips will help you master it:

*   **Cold Water is Key**: Using very cold water (or even sparkling water) for the batter helps create a crispier pancake by inhibiting gluten development.
*   **Don't Skimp on Oil**: Haemul Pajeon is meant to be pan-fried, not just lightly greased. A generous amount of oil is crucial for achieving that irresistible crispy crust.
*   **Medium-High Heat**: Ensure your pan is hot enough before pouring the batter. Too low, and the pancake will absorb too much oil and become greasy; too high, and it will burn before cooking through.
*   **Spread Thinly**: For maximum crispiness, aim for a relatively thin pancake. A thicker pancake will be chewier.
*   **Press Down**: After flipping, gently press down on the pancake with your spatula. This ensures even cooking and helps create a uniform crispy surface.
*   **Resting the Batter**: Letting the batter rest for 10-15 minutes after mixing can help hydrate the flours, resulting in a slightly more tender pancake.

## Serving Suggestions & Dipping Sauce

Haemul Pajeon is typically served immediately after cooking, while it’s still hot and crispy. Cut it into wedge-shaped pieces or squares with a pizza cutter or knife.

The dipping sauce is an integral part of the experience. To make the classic dipping sauce, simply combine all the dipping sauce ingredients listed above in a small bowl and stir well. Taste and adjust to your preference – add more soy sauce for saltiness, vinegar for tang, or gochugaru for heat.

Serve your Haemul Pajeon as an appetizer alongside other Korean dishes like kimchi, pickled radishes, or tteokbokki. It also makes a fantastic light lunch or dinner when paired with a simple bowl of rice. A cold beer or makgeolli (Korean rice wine) is the perfect beverage to accompany this savory delight.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the traditional Haemul Pajeon is a classic for a reason, don't hesitate to experiment with variations to suit your taste or what you have on hand:

*   **Vegetarian Pajeon (Yachaejeon)**: Omit the seafood and increase the amount of vegetables. Sliced mushrooms (shiitake, oyster), carrots, zucchini, or even kimchi can be wonderful additions.
*   **Kimchi Pajeon (Kimchijeon)**: A very popular variation where chopped kimchi is mixed into the batter. Squeeze excess liquid from the kimchi before adding it.
*   **Beef Pajeon (Soegogi Pajeon)**: Add thinly sliced marinated beef (like bulgogi meat) instead of or in addition to seafood for a richer flavor.
*   **Different Seafood**: Feel free to use your favorite seafood combination. Scallops, small fish fillets, or even imitation crab meat can work well.
*   **Gluten-Free**: Experiment with a gluten-free flour blend for the batter, ensuring it has a good starch component to maintain crispiness.

Haemul Pajeon is more than just a pancake; it's a celebration of fresh ingredients, simple techniques, and incredible flavor. Whether you’re new to Korean cuisine or a seasoned fan, making this iconic dish at home is a rewarding experience that brings a taste of Korea right to your kitchen. Enjoy the delightful crunch, the tender seafood, and the aromatic green onions in every bite!
